hub_subtitle: Business English para seguros, reuniões e entrevistas — e o desbloqueio da fala, do primeiro dia ao último

lesson.menu_title: My Routine, My World -- Daily Life in English
lesson.menu_desc: O primeiro verbo de ação: seis verbos da rotina (work, start, finish, travel, meet, talk) e o present simple na 1ª pessoa -- I work / I don't work / Do you travel? -- até descrever um dia inteiro na Zurich, do "I start at nine" ao "I don't work on weekends." -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- My Routine, My World
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Paula Morales | Lesson 3 -- My Routine, My World
lesson.grammar_point: present simple -- 1st person (I work / I don't work / Do you...?)

listenings.text: Hi, Paula. This is David. Welcome to your first week. Let me tell you about my day here. I start at eight o'clock. I have a coffee, and I read my emails. At nine, I talk to my team. I usually meet two or three clients every day. I travel on Tuesdays — I visit brokers in Rio. I don't work on weekends. Never. Your first meeting is on Monday, at ten. See you there. Goodbye.

Good morning, Paula. Sofia here. You ask me about my week — so, here it is. I start at nine, and I finish at six. Every morning I talk to my team for ten minutes. Ten minutes, no more. I meet clients on Wednesdays and Thursdays. I travel once a month — I visit our office in Lima. And I don't answer emails after seven. That is my rule. Your routine is your rule too. Welcome, Paula.

items.situation: A new colleague asks: "What time do you start?" Answer with a full sentence — and add when you finish.
"What do you do at Zurich?" Say two things you do — not what you are.
tips: I meet clients and I talk to brokers.

items.situation: "Do you work on Saturdays?" You don't. Answer with the short answer, then the full sentence.
"Why do you travel?" Say the reason, in one sentence.
tips: I visit brokers in Rio and in Campinas.

items.situation: "Do you travel every day?" You travel two days a week. Say no — and then say the truth.
tips: No, I don't. Not every day.
I usually travel on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

items.situation: You want to know the other person's routine. Ask a question with "Do you".
tips: Do you meet clients too? / What time do you start?

bank.label: My day / O meu dia
items: I start at nine o'clock and I finish at six.
I usually meet clients and I talk to brokers.

bank.label: Asking and answering / Perguntar e responder
items: Yes, I do. / No, I don't.
Not every day — two days a week.
I don't speak perfect English — yet.
